response:
```python
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, call

def test_retry_happy_path():
    """Test that retry works correctly when function succeeds on first attempt"""
    mock_fn = Mock(return_value="success")
    
    result = retry(mock_fn)
    
    assert result == "success"
    mock_fn.assert_called_once()

def test_retry_succeeds_on_second_attempt():
    """Test that retry works when function fails first but succeeds on retry"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=[Exception("fail"), "success"])
    
    result = retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=3)
    
    assert result == "success"
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 2

def test_retry_succeeds_on_last_attempt():
    """Test that retry works when function succeeds on the final attempt"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=[Exception("fail1"), Exception("fail2"), "success"])
    
    result = retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=3)
    
    assert result == "success"
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 3

def test_retry_raises_exception_on_failure():
    """Test that retry raises the last exception when all attempts fail"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=[Exception("fail1"), Exception("fail2"), Exception("fail3")])
    
    with pytest.raises(Exception, match="fail3"):
        retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=3)
    
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 3

def test_retry_default_max_attempts():
    """Test that retry uses default max_attempts of 3"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=[Exception("fail1"), Exception("fail2"), Exception("fail3")])
    
    with pytest.raises(Exception, match="fail3"):
        retry(mock_fn)  # Should default to 3 attempts
    
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 3

def test_retry_single_attempt():
    """Test retry with max_attempts=1 (no retries)"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=Exception("fail"))
    
    with pytest.raises(Exception, match="fail"):
        retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=1)
    
    mock_fn.assert_called_once()

def test_retry_zero_attempts():
    """Test retry with max_attempts=0"""
    mock_fn = Mock(return_value="success")
    
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=0)
    
    mock_fn.assert_not_called()

def test_retry_with_different_exception_types():
    """Test that retry handles different types of exceptions"""
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=[ValueError("value error"), TypeError("type error"), "success"])
    
    result = retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=5)
    
    assert result == "success"
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 3

def test_retry_propagates_last_exception():
    """Test that the exact last exception is propagated, not a wrapper"""
    class CustomException(Exception):
        pass
    
    mock_fn = Mock(side_effect=CustomException("custom error"))
    
    with pytest.raises(CustomException, match="custom error"):
        retry(mock_fn, max_attempts=2)
    
    assert mock_fn.call_count == 2
```
